Google Search Title:
====================

egfr normal range

  

Google Search AI Overview:
==========================

  

![eGFR (Glomerular Filtration Rate) Test + How To Improve ...](data:image/png;base64,)

A normal eGFR (estimated glomerular filtration rate) is generally considered to be 90 mL/min/1.73 m

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAP///wAAACH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

2squared

2

or higher. However, healthy kidney function eGFR values naturally decline with age, and a result between 60–89 mL/min/1.73 m

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAP///wAAACH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

2squared

2

is often completely normal in the absence of other signs of kidney damage. [](https://www.kidney.org/kidney-topics/estimated-glomerular-filtration-rate-egfr)

Understanding exactly where your lab results fall can help you track your kidney health over time. [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwzzaZsPA9w&t=43)

eGFR Result Ranges & What They Mean 

Kidney function is typically measured in milliliters per minute per 1.73 

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAP///wAAACH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw==)

m2m squared

𝑚2

of body surface area. [](https://www.kidneyresearchuk.org/kidney-health-information/living-with-kidney-disease/how-can-i-help-myself/understanding-test-results/)

*   **90 or higher:**
    
    Considered normal kidney function.
    

*   **60 to 89:** Generally normal, assuming there are no other symptoms or evidence of kidney disease (such as protein in the urine).
*   **45 to 59:** Mild to moderate loss of kidney function.
*   **30 to 44:** Moderate to severe loss of kidney function.
*   **15 to 29:** Severe loss of kidney function.
*   **Less than 15:** Kidney failure, requiring dialysis or a transplant. [](https://www.kidney.org/kidney-topics/estimated-glomerular-filtration-rate-egfr)

Important Considerations 

*   **Natural Age Decline:** eGFR naturally decreases by about 1 mL/min per year as you get older, even in healthy adults. [](https://www.kidney.org.uk/chronic-kidney-disease)
*   **Other Markers:** Doctors usually look at eGFR alongside a urine test checking for albumin (a type of protein) to fully assess kidney health. [](https://digital.nhs.uk/data-and-information/publications/statistical/health-survey-for-england/2022-part-2/kidney-disease)
*   **Accuracy limitations:** eGFR calculations are estimates and may be less reliable if you are very muscular, are an amputee, have had a rapid change in kidney function (like acute kidney injury), or are pregnant. [](https://www.ukkidney.org/health-professionals/information-resources/uk-eckd-guide/measurement-kidney-function)

For official guidance and detailed information on staging, you can check the [UK Kidney Association](https://www.ukkidney.org/health-professionals/information-resources/uk-eckd-guide/ckd-staging) or the [National Kidney Foundation](https://www.kidney.org/kidney-topics/estimated-glomerular-filtration-rate-egfr).
